description_long: This object consists of two tall, slender stone obelisks standing side by side on a rectangular patch of green mossy ground. Each obelisk is mounted on a square pedestal base, with one appearing more weathered and discolored than the other. The surfaces of both obelisks show signs of age, with visible stains, erosion, and some chipping, especially on the left obelisk. Faint inscriptions are visible on the faces of both obelisks, though they are difficult to read due to weathering. The tips of the obelisks are pointed and sharply defined, typical of traditional memorial or commemorative monuments. The surrounding area is covered in dense, uneven green moss, suggesting an outdoor, possibly historical or park-like setting.
description: A pair of weathered stone obelisks with pointed tips stand on mossy ground, each mounted on a square pedestal and bearing faint inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view showing both obelisks side by side, with visible inscriptions and weathering on their faces.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the obelisks and the mossy ground extending behind them.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both obelisks, with more pronounced discoloration and surface wear on the left obelisk.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the slim silhouette of the right obelisk and the mossy base.
attribute_inscription: Faint, weathered inscriptions are visible on the front faces of both obelisks, in a serifed style, likely in English, but largely illegible due to erosion.
attribute_base_design: Each obelisk stands on a square, tiered stone pedestal with simple, unadorned edges and no visible decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: Both obelisks have sharply pointed, pyramidal tips with no additional decoration or embellishment.
attribute_surrounding_features: Dense, uneven green moss covers the ground around the obelisks, with no other notable features or objects present.
